name: compliance_affirming_with_nc_status
description:
Pins the THIRD cross-status mismatch direction in the STATUS_NARRATIVE_MISMATCH cross-status branch at engine/validator.py:481-490, completing matrix coverage. The branch is symmetric -- `if status is not None and status != expected_status` -- so a regression in ANY direction would silently flip one cell while leaving others passing. Sibling cases:
  - status_narrative_mismatch_cross_status: GAP_DESCRIBING narrative + COMPLIANT status (LLM writes gap language but marks Compliant). expected_status=NON_COMPLIANT, actual=COMPLIANT.
  - status_narrative_mismatch_na_with_affirming: COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING narrative + NOT_APPLICABLE status (LLM writes assessment-act language but marks NA). expected_status=COMPLIANT, actual=NOT_APPLICABLE.
  - THIS CASE: COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING narrative + NON_COMPLIANT status. expected_status=COMPLIANT, actual=NON_COMPLIANT. The LLM retrieves a comprehensive plan document, reflexively writes affirming prose proving the control IS implemented, then mislabels the verdict as Non-Compliant -- perhaps because a separate prompt token nudged it toward NC. Without this case, breaking only the COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING-vs-NC direction would let both sibling cases still pass and a misclassified NC verdict could ship.

Three-by-three cross-status matrix coverage status now:
  - GAP_DESCRIBING actual: vs COMPLIANT (covered), vs NOT_APPLICABLE (not yet covered), vs NON_COMPLIANT (correct, no rejection).
  - COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING actual: vs COMPLIANT (correct, no rejection), vs NOT_APPLICABLE (covered), vs NON_COMPLIANT (THIS CASE).
  - NA_JUSTIFYING actual: vs any non-NA status (not yet covered; future slice).
Two of the three remaining gap-cells are NA_JUSTIFYING-narrative mismatches and one is GAP_DESCRIBING-vs-NA. Those are lower-frequency LLM failure modes; the COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING gaps are the realistic ones a 3PAO would catch.

Mechanism trace:
  - status = NON_COMPLIANT.
  - Narrative contains three _AFFIRMING_PHRASES (validator.py:43-60): 'Examined ' (line 57), 'confirmed via' (line 44), 'documented in' (line 54).
  - Narrative contains zero _NA_PHRASES (validator.py:63-80): no 'not applicable because', no 'implemented by aws/azure/gcp/csp', no 'outside system boundary'.
  - Narrative contains zero _GAP_PHRASES (validator.py:93-113): no 'no documentation', no 'POA&M', no 'remediation', no 'missing', no 'has not been'.
  - classes_hit = 1 (affirming only), so classify_narrative returns COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING unambiguously (not AMBIGUOUS, which would route to the OTHER branch at validator.py:471-480).
  - _expected_status_for_class(COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING) returns COMPLIANT (validator.py:736-737).
  - status (NON_COMPLIANT) != expected_status (COMPLIANT) -> cross-status branch fires at validator.py:481-490.
  - Three identical proposals exhaust the retry budget -> _abstain with reason 'validator-exhausted: ... status_narrative_mismatch ...'.
  - Per Fix 3 (KERNEL_VERSION 0.5.0): hard-abstain coerces status to None and preserves the LLM's intended verdict on proposed_status. expected.proposed_status='Non-Compliant' guards that coercion path for the NC direction (sibling cases pin it for COMPLIANT and NOT_APPLICABLE directions).

Load-bearing details that ISOLATE the cross-status branch from cascades:
  - Narrative does NOT match _RESTATEMENT_REGEXES (validator.py:117-122). Opens with 'Examined USD00033333' -- none of the four restatement openers fire.
  - Narrative token-set Jaccard vs procedures stays well below _OVERLAP_THRESHOLD=0.70. Procedures: 'Examine documented security awareness training requirements and confirm coverage of all personnel categories.' Narrative tokens (after _TOKEN_RE filter at validator.py:779 [a-z0-9]{3,}): examined, usd00033333, example-system, security, awareness, training, plan, rev, confirmed, via, section, annual, requirements, are, documented, the, plan, for, all, personnel, categories -- ~21 distinct. Procedures tokens: examine, documented, security, awareness, training, requirements, and, confirm, coverage, all, personnel, categories -- ~12 distinct. Intersection: security, awareness, training, requirements, documented, all, personnel, categories -- 8 tokens. Jaccard = 8/25 = 0.32, below 0.70. _is_requirement_restatement returns False -> REQUIREMENT_RESTATEMENT does NOT cascade.
  - Narrative does NOT match _FUTURE_TENSE_RE (validator.py:187-198). All verbs present-tense ('are documented'). Future-tense gate at validator.py:541-555 also requires status=COMPLIANT, so it would skip on NC status anyway -- belt-and-suspenders.
  - No 'inherited from' string -> MISSING_INHERITANCE_MARKER skipped (validator.py:508-516).
  - No SV-#####r#_rule citation -> UNCORROBORATED_STIG_PASS skipped (validator.py:570-587); also requires status=COMPLIANT to fire, which doesn't apply here.
  - USD00033333 IS in tagged_evidence so cite-verifier (validator.py:_verify_cites at 858) passes -- the only USD token in the narrative is USD00033333 and it appears literally in evidence_text. UNSUPPORTED_DOC_CITATION does NOT cascade.
  - The GAP_DESCRIBING + NON_COMPLIANT POA&M note at validator.py:521-530 does NOT fire here because the narrative is COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING, not GAP_DESCRIBING. Even if it did fire, it appends a NOTE not a rejection, so wouldn't pollute rejection_classes.
  - No hybrid-split markers (no 'On-prem:', 'Cloud:', '## responsibility_split'). _HYBRID_SPLIT_RE at validator.py:140-142 does not match, so the hybrid-merge path is skipped and classify_narrative runs the single-narrative path that gives the clean COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING classification we need.
  - COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING + primary-citation NOTE at validator.py:495-502: USD00033333 IS a primary-source citation (USD doc) so the helper note about missing primary citation does NOT fire. Even if it did, it's a NOTE not a REJECTION.

Three identical proposals (not three variants): the stub does not consume corrective_context, so the orchestrator's job is just to keep retrying until the budget is gone. Same retry-exhaustion contract pinned by status_narrative_mismatch_cross_status / status_narrative_mismatch_na_with_affirming / future_tense_rejection / unsupported_doc_citation / uncorroborated_stig_pass_rejection.

Control AT-2.1 / CCI-000100 (security awareness training) substrate is realistic: the program team has a Security Awareness Training Plan document AND annual training records, but the LLM retrieves only the plan, writes 'Examined ... confirmed via ... documented in ...' affirming prose, then over-corrects to Non-Compliant because the prompt also nudged it about missing completion records. The narrative says 'yes implemented', the status says 'no it isn't' -- exactly the failure mode the cross-status branch was written to catch in the NC direction.

ccis_row.guidance: The organization provides basic security awareness training to information system users (including managers, senior executives, and contractors) as part of initial training for new users.
ccis_row.procedures: Examine documented security awareness training requirements and confirm coverage of all personnel categories.

tagged_evidence:
## Tagged evidence
- USD00033333 Example System Security Awareness Training Plan Rev C — documents the annual training program, designated personnel categories, and the cadence at which initial and refresher training is delivered.

llm_stub_proposals.narrative: Examined USD00033333 Example System Security Awareness Training Plan Rev C; confirmed via section 2 that annual training requirements are documented in the plan for all personnel categories.
Examined USD00033333 Example System Security Awareness Training Plan Rev C; confirmed via section 2 that annual training requirements are documented in the plan for all personnel categories.
Examined USD00033333 Example System Security Awareness Training Plan Rev C; confirmed via section 2 that annual training requirements are documented in the plan for all personnel categories.
